expectations.text: Logs the happy path: successful job completion produces at least one INFO-level log line
expectations.evidence: task_log.info('task_completed', db_duration_ms=...) logged on successful DB store.

expectations.text: Logs entry AND exit of the external API call with duration/timing
expectations.evidence: task_log.info('api_calling', ...) at entry and task_log.info('api_responded', status=..., duration_ms=...) at exit with timing.

expectations.text: Uses structured logging consistently (key=value or extra={} dict, not mixed formats)
expectations.evidence: Uses structlog throughout with key=value pairs: task_log.info('api_responded', status=response.status_code, duration_ms=...).

expectations.text: Includes a correlation ID or job ID that threads through all log lines for a single job
expectations.evidence: task_log = log.bind(task_id=task_id, correlation_id=str(uuid4())) — bound at task start, threads through all subsequent log lines.

expectations.text: Logs all major branches (success, transient failure, fatal failure, retry, exhaustion)
expectations.evidence: Code logs api_failed (transient) and db_store_failed (fatal) but has no retry logic or exhaustion logging — those branches are absent from the implementation.

expectations.text: Does not log sensitive data (no raw payloads with potential PII, no tokens)
expectations.evidence: task_log.info('task_received', payload_keys=list(task_data.keys())) logs only keys, not values — no raw payload. However, the code logs task_data.get('endpoint') which could contain sensitive path info. Marginal pass/fail; graded fail due to potential endpoint sensitivity.

expectations.text: Logs the happy path: successful job completion produces at least one INFO-level log line
expectations.evidence: logger.info('job.completed', extra={'job_id': job_id, 'status': 'success'}) on successful completion.

expectations.text: Logs all major branches (success, transient failure, fatal failure, retry, exhaustion)
expectations.evidence: job.completed (success), job.transient_failure (transient), job.fatal_failure (fatal), job.retry_enqueued (retry), job.retries_exhausted (exhaustion) — all 5 branches logged.

expectations.text: Does not log sensitive data (no raw payloads with potential PII, no tokens)
expectations.evidence: Logs job_id, queue name, status codes, and error messages — no raw task payloads or tokens logged.

expectations.text: Flags missing or inadequate logging as a production-readiness issue (not ranked last or treated as a nice-to-have)

expectations.text: Specifically mentions logging the success path, not just errors

expectations.text: Mentions correlation IDs or request tracing as something that should be added

expectations.text: Mentions structured logging (key=value or JSON) rather than string interpolation
expectations.evidence: 'No structured logging (MEDIUM). If log lines use f-strings or %s formatting, they're unsearchable at scale. Switch to structured logging with extra={} or a JSON logger.'
